name: microsoft-sdl
tagline: Security is a phase-gate, not a review comment.
philosophy: Security requirements are woven into every phase: threat-model at design time, blocking static analysis, secrets hygiene every cycle. Vulnerabilities are never traded off against features.

gates.description: Any change touching auth, input parsing, crypto, or network handling requires a threat-model note from the builder: assets, entry points, what an attacker controls, mitigations.
Security-class static findings (injection, path traversal, insecure deserialization, weak crypto) are always blocking, never advisory.
Every cycle: no credentials, tokens, or keys in the diff; no secrets moved into code from config.
Any manifest change gets a known-vulnerability check (using the project's existing audit tooling, e.g. npm audit / pip-audit / cargo audit, when present).
A security finding cannot be waived for feature progress; it is fixed or the run halts with the finding escalated.
